title: ZooKeeper Outstanding Requests Queue Depth
dataSources: `echo mntr | nc localhost 2181` output ingested as `zookeeper:mntr`
app: Scripted input or Telegraf `exec` calling four-letter `mntr` (enable `4lw.commands.whitelist=mntr` in `zoo.cfg`)
spl:
index=infrastructure sourcetype="zookeeper:mntr"
| eval out=tonumber(zk_outstanding_requests)
| where out > 1000
| timechart span=1m max(out) as outstanding by host
description: The outstanding requests metric is the fastest indicator that the ensemble cannot dequeue client operations quickly enough—usually CPU saturation, storage latency on txn logs, or a noisy neighbor client.
value: Stops coordinated services from timing out en masse when ZK request latency climbs.
implementation: Poll `mntr` every 10–30s; parse `zk_outstanding_requests`, `zk_avg_latency`, `zk_server_state`. Use dedicated monitoring user and firewall rules. Alert >1000 sustained 5 minutes (tune to cluster size).
